summary: Discovered 4 verified candidates and 5 rejections for MIT. The primary AI-specific policy sources are: (1) IS&T's GenAI guidance page covering data privacy, transparency requirements, and risk classifications; (2) TLL's teaching guidance on GenAI in courses; (3) TLL's blog post on rethinking problem sets with GenAI; (4) IS&T's approved AI tools catalog. Rejected sources include general academic integrity policies (Mind and Hand Book, Academic Integrity Handbook, research misconduct policy 10.1) that do not explicitly mention AI/GenAI, and a library citation guide that is a reference list rather than policy. Web search was unavailable (provider disabled), limiting discovery to operator-seeded URLs and content-derived links. Key observation: MIT's approach is layered — IT/security guidance from IS&T, teaching guidance from TLL, with the general academic integrity framework (handbook, policies.mit.edu) serving as the backdrop without AI-specific provisions.
